title: scutellaria rupestris subsp. rupestris
intro: Scutellaria rupestris subsp. rupestris, also known as alpine skullcap, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, Scutellaria. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily due to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of alpine skullcap include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and support immune function, as well as its traditional use in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. Alpine skullcap can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively studied in clinical trials.
